description: Seed-robustness / stability of the foundational domain recovery (a-0001/a-0002), which rests on ONE Leiden run (seed 42). Re-run Leiden (ModularityVertexPartition, 10 iterations) on the EXACT object cluster.py uses (undirected LCC, 92,594 nodes / 308,095 edges) under 7 seeds {0,1,7,42,123,2024,31337}. Measure per-seed n_clusters & modularity; ARI/NMI vs the canonical stored partition (data/clusters.json); pairwise ARI/NMI across seeds (run-to-run agreement); and NMI vs top-level Mathlib module labels (the domain-recovery metric, anchored to a-0002's 0.307).
motivation: The whole paper rests on a single stochastic Leiden run; a-0001/a-0002 claim unsupervised clustering recovers Mathlib's named domains but no experiment checks whether that recovery — or the exact partition the bridge analysis depends on — survives a seed change. Cheap CPU-only reproducibility check over existing data.
formal_results: 7 seeds on the LCC (92,594 nodes, 308,095 edges). Per-seed NMI vs top-level module: 0.281-0.289, mean 0.2847 +/- 0.0025 (anchor a-0002=0.307). Modularity 0.618-0.627 (tight). n_clusters mean 140.6 +/- 37.3, range 93-223. ARI vs stored partition 0.416-0.624; NMI vs stored 0.583-0.667. Pairwise across seeds: ARI mean 0.465 (min 0.384), NMI mean 0.588 (min 0.546).
results_description: Domain-recovery signal is highly stable: NMI vs Mathlib modules is 0.2847 +/- 0.0025 across all 7 seeds (essentially constant), and modularity is tight (0.618-0.627). But the EXACT partition is only moderately reproducible: cluster count swings 93-223 and pairwise run-to-run ARI averages just 0.465. The canonical stored partition (138 clusters) is itself only moderately recovered by fresh reruns (ARI 0.42-0.62), and a fresh seed-42 run yields 223 clusters, not the stored 138 — so cluster.py's stored partition reflects extra processing/selection, not a bare reproducible Leiden run.
conclusions: Strengthens a-0001/a-0002 at the aggregate level while flagging a real caveat. The foundational domain-recovery claim (clusters agree with Mathlib modules) is seed-ROBUST: module-NMI varies by <1% across seeds. However, cluster-IDENTITY-dependent downstream claims — the specific 34 bridge theorems, the exact 138 clusters, SetTheory-as-hub by cluster id — rest on one particular partition whose membership and even cluster COUNT (93-223) shift substantially across seeds (pairwise ARI ~0.46). Aggregate module structure is stable; the fine partition is not. Caveat: ModularityVertexPartition has no resolution parameter, so count variation is intrinsic Leiden stochasticity.
